title: Outdoor Chair (Modern Comfort Collection)
summary: A slatted-back 2x4 armchair with genuine arm supports and a slanted backrest — a real armchair silhouette, unlike this catalog's cushion-based or dining-height outdoor chairs.
description:
Four 2x4 legs carry the seat frame, with 2x6 arm supports and 2x4 arm supports layered together at each side to hold the arm rests at a comfortable height — this layered support is what gives the arms real heft rather than reading as a thin cushion-chair frame. Attach the base pieces first, then the legs, before adding anything above seat height.

**The backrest is genuinely slatted, not a single cushioned panel** — four 2x4 back slats run between the back base and the backrest top, angled back slightly for real lounging comfort rather than a bolt-upright dining seat. This slatted-back-plus-armrest combination is what separates this chair from this catalog's cushion-first and dining-height outdoor chair designs.

Seat slat cleats support four 1x4 seat slats, spaced for drainage so the chair doesn't hold rainwater. Use exterior-rated screws and a moisture-resistant finish throughout, since this chair is built to live outside.

materials.name: Exterior primer and paint, or stain

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut four legs at 22", two arm rests at 34", two arm supports, narrow at 31", one back at 25-1/2" and four back slats at 23-3/4" from the 2x4. Cut two arm supports, wide at 31", two bases at 25-1/2" and one back base at 29-1/2" from the 2x6. Cut one backrest top at 25-1/2" and two seat slat cleats at 29-1/2" from the 2x2. Cut four seat slats at 23-1/2" from the 1x4. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to four 8 ft 2x4, two 8 ft 2x6, one 8 ft 2x2 and one 8 ft 1x4. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.

steps.title: Build the base and legs
steps.body: Assemble the two bases and attach the four legs.

steps.title: Add the arm supports and arm rests
steps.body: Layer the narrow and wide arm supports at each side and attach the arm rests on top.

steps.title: Build the backrest
steps.body: Attach the back, backrest top, back base, and four angled back slats to form the slatted backrest.

steps.title: Add the seat
steps.body: Attach the seat slat cleats, then space and attach the four seat slats for drainage.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply exterior-rated primer and paint, or stain.
materials: Exterior primer and paint, or stain

unresolvedImages.alt: Outdoor armchair with slatted back and arm rests
